approach: CR fully specifies the target contract for both verbs, so the design needs no invention
suite has zero send/submit scenarios and zero @frozen tags at node status draft, so suite work is purely additive — no narrowing, no Clearance floor
breaking SessionAdapter + CLI change is authorized by the CR itself and the package is pre-1.0, so the Compatibility ceiling is not exceeded
no plugin registry present — SDD default production chain for every touched artifact-type

summary: Decide whether the shared AXI content-first principle should carve out a command group with no live view of its own
detail: The shared CLI-output contract says a command group invoked with no subcommand shows live data rather than help. This project's send group is its first command group and does not satisfy that: every view it could derive already belongs to an existing verb (the pane enumeration verb, and the probe verb that reports the current pane), and its key vocabulary is a spec constant rather than live data — so binding any of them to the bare form would ship a second name for a shipped command. This project's bare send therefore writes help to the error stream and exits 1, the framework default and the same shape the contract's fail-loud principle already blesses for an unknown flag; no new exit code is introduced. The question of whether the principle should carve out such a group belongs to the contract, which is shared with two other adopters, not to this project alone. This project states only its own behavior and asserts nothing about theirs. Whoever picks this up should check each adopter's actual binary rather than its spec node — the two can differ.
why-not-blocking: the amendment is self-contained and explicitly scoped; no completion claim this mission made depends on the siblings adopting it

why: cold spec-judge three-lens all PASS, ALIGNED true, zero failing scenarios, no open markers; mechanical suite + spec-state checks green. Purely additive at draft with zero prior frozen scenarios, so no narrowing and no Clearance floor; the breaking contract change is authorized by the CR on a pre-1.0 package. Judge independently re-probed every load-bearing backend claim against both live backends and reproduced the wrong-execution fault the CR exists to prevent. Frozen mux.feature; node advances to approved.

summary: Surface backend failures to the caller — the command-runner seam currently swallows them, so a refused key reports success
detail: The synchronous command-runner every adapter takes discards the child process's error stream and reports a failed command as a null return, which the pane-driving verbs ignore. So when one backend refuses a key it does not know, the CLI still exits 0 and prints nothing: the caller cannot distinguish a refused key from a pressed one. This is not specific to the key verbs — it is a property of the seam and affects every verb that issues a backend command. Found at this mission's implementation gate, where the judge showed that a prose claim about the refusal being loud is true at the backend boundary and false from where a caller sits. Scoping this properly means deciding what a caller-observable backend failure looks like across the whole verb surface, including the structured-error and exit-code conventions the project's output contract already specifies but does not yet implement.
why-not-blocking: pre-existing property of the seam, not introduced by this mission; no frozen scenario asserts a caller-observable outcome for the refusal case, and the one that covers it is written about the backend's behavior, which is verified true

why: cold impl-judge re-derived every CR-added frozen scenario independently and verified each against both live backends by driving the built binary and reading panes back, rather than trusting the mocked command-runner; it also demonstrated its probe catches the pre-change fault before confirming the fault is gone. Repo verification green, full suite passing, no absorption finding, no structural blocker, no scenario without a verification. The judge's substantive finding — a prose claim of loudness that the command-runner seam does not deliver to a caller — was corrected in place and filed as a follow-up rather than shipped. Node advances to implemented.
